assistant.generated_text: Gravity is a fundamental force of nature that attracts objects with mass towards each other. It was first scientifically described by Sir Isaac Newton (1643-1727) in his groundbreaking work "Philosophiæ Naturalis Principia Mathematica" published in 1687. If you're interested in learning more about this topic, I'd recommend exploring physics education resources online!

Gravity is a fundamental force of nature that causes objects with mass to attract each other.

Earth's gravity pulls us towards its center, keeping our feet on the ground.
